As soon as he finished his specialization studies in France, Dr. Juvenal Urbino became known in the country for having conjured in time, with novel and drastic methods, the last epidemic of morbid cholera that the province suffered.
The previous one, when he was still in Europe, had caused death to a quarter of the urban population in less than three months, including his father, who was also a very appreciated doctor.

With the immediate prestige and a good contribution to the family heritage, he founded the medical society, the first and the only one in the Caribbean provinces for many years, and was its life president.
He achieved the construction of the first aqueduct, the first sewer system, and the covered public market that allowed the pudder of the Bay of the Ánimas.

The Latin Patriarch of Jerusalem made him a gentleman of the order of the Holy Sepulcher for his services to the Church, and the government of France granted him the Legion of Honor in the degree of Commander.
